question.text: How often must the AMC update the SID?

answer.text: At least once every half-year (within one month from end of each half-year)
Only when the scheme is launched

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** SEBI requires AMCs to update the SID within one month from the end of each half-year (based on data as at end of September and March respectively). This means the SID is effectively updated at least twice a year. Material changes must also be updated immediately.
**The Trap:** The SID is NOT a one-time document created only at launch. It is a living document that must be updated regularly. At the same time, monthly updates are not required — that frequency applies to fact sheets.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Also tested in Series V-A. The annual update requirement is a regulatory mandate.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always check the date on the SID to make sure you are referring to the latest version. An outdated SID may have incorrect information about loads, benchmarks, or fund managers.
